threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You don't yet know the 'secret' shortcuts through the backstreets. You'll still be on the main roads while locals glide past, grinning.
Heard a guy on the N37 bus bragging about faking a phone call to avoid a drunk chap trying to sit next to him. Classic Brixton night antics.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sipping a coffee on Hermannplatz, I watched a kid juggle oranges while a busker played the accordion so out of tune it felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you find a club that doubles as a skate park. It’s the perfect blend of nightlife and daytime adrenaline, all under one roof.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local if they instinctively know which taco truck is worth the wait at 3 AM, not just the one with the longest line.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Last week, a guy on the 47 bus serenaded us with a ukulele while we waited for the next stop. Classic Muni, unexpected and bizarre.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí. They miss the real gems hidden in the little plazas, where the locals actually play petanca.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their bewildered faces at the Palau de la Música; they’re the ones trying to read the schedule and ending up in the wrong place.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think 'the Tube' is the best way to get around. Not realizing the Overground is your new best friend.
Caught a bloke singing opera on the 355 last night. Crowds went from groaning to cheering in seconds. Brixton's night life never disappoints.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I heard a street musician playing accordion while a couple argued about their cat on the corner. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you grab a late-night Döner while debating if the U-Bahn will actually run on time during a strike. It's a wild mix of chaos and comfort.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they know to avoid Civic Center when BART’s down; tourists just stand around confused while we pass them by.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a street performer using the Muni bus stop as a stage while juggling flaming torches. Only in the Tenderloin would that be a Tuesday vibe.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about Gaudí, but locals know it’s the little corner bars and nightly jam sessions that give it life.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they ask for a 'café con leche' instead of just a 'café'. It’s the little things that give them away.
